column5: Essential for proprioception, which provides the gravity reference frame for posture control.
column6: Loss of function mutations cause congenital scoliosis; asymmetric expression in paraspinal muscles in AIS. (DOI: 10.1056/NEJMoa1602812)
column8: Direct link between gravity sensing (proprioception) and spinal alignment.

column5: Regulates migration of muscle precursors and formation of dorsal horn neurons involved in proprioception/gravity sensing.
column6: Top GWAS hit for Adolescent Idiopathic Scoliosis (AIS). (DOI: 10.1038/ng.814)
column8: Major genetic driver of AIS with clear mechanistic link to postural tone.

column5: Essential for development of proprioceptive neurons (gravity sensing).
column6: Null mutants develop scoliosis due to loss of proprioceptive input. (Blecher et al., 2017)
column8: Primary driver of proprioceptive-dependent spinal alignment.

column5: Essential for development of proprioceptive neurons (gravity sensing).
column6: Loss of function mutations cause congenital scoliosis due to lack of proprioceptive input. (Blecher et al., 2017)
column8: Primary driver of proprioceptive-dependent spinal alignment.

column5: Nuclear lamina scales with tissue stiffness; central mechanotransducer (Swift et al., 2013).
column6: Mutations cause LMNA-CMD and EDMD2/3 with severe scoliosis. (PMC4105307)
column8: Direct link between nuclear mechanotransduction and spinal stability.

column5: Neurofibromin regulates Ras/MAPK; loss alters cytoskeletal contractility and mechanosensitivity.
column6: Dystrophic scoliosis is a hallmark of Neurofibromatosis Type 1. (DOI: 10.5435/JAAOS-D-19-00105)
column8: Direct causative link to dystrophic scoliosis and mechanotransduction.

column5: Regulates ER calcium handling and redox state; essential for muscle excitation-contraction coupling under load.
column6: Mutations cause Rigid Spine Muscular Dystrophy (RSMD1); defining feature is severe spinal rigidity and scoliosis. (DOI: 10.1016/S1474-4422(02)00005-1)
column8: Defining gene for 'Rigid Spine' phenotype; links ER stress to postural tone.

column5: Required for muscle spindle morphogenesis (gravity/stretch sensors).
column6: Knockout mice lack muscle spindles and develop scoliosis. (Blecher et al., 2017)
column8: Essential for the hardware of muscle proprioception.

column5: Forms proton channels in otoconia; essential for otolith formation (gravity sensors).
column6: Vestibular dysfunction is strongly linked to Idiopathic Scoliosis onset. (DOI: 10.1007/s40141-013-0026-6)
column8: Essential for the hardware of vestibular gravity sensing.

column5: Hydroxylates lysines for collagen crosslinking; determines mechanical stability.
column6: Kyphoscoliosis type Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome (EDS VIA) is caused by PLOD1 deficiency. (DOI: 10.1056/NEJM197206222862503)
column8: Defining gene for Kyphoscoliotic EDS; major structural determinant.
